page: Microsoft Azure D13 is a D-family virtual machine instance powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. The CPU includes a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache. It is equipped with 56.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 7.0 GB per core. The instance also includes 410 GB of local storage, while lacking any bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is generally weak, as evidenced by bottom-tier results in stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, the instance achieves average, middle-tier performance in multi-core metrics, including stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks remains in the average tier, alongside average performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput.

The primary tradeoff of the Azure D13 instance lies in its modest single-threaded execution speed compared to its more capable multi-threaded performance and generous memory allocation. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density of 7.0 GB per core makes it a viable option for workloads requiring moderate memory footprints without the premium cost of dedicated memory-optimized families. It is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded software compilation, web serving, and moderate database operations that benefit from the 410 GB of local storage and balanced multi-core throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure D13 is a D-family virtual machine instance configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 56.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.0 GB per core, and includes 410 GB of local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance lacks GPU accelerators. Benchmark data reveals a performance profile where single-core execution is weak, but multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform at an average level. Qualitatively, the D13 offers a balanced resource density, making it cost-efficient for workloads requiring more memory than standard general-purpose instances without transitioning to expensive memory-optimized tiers. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate database workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D13 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 56 GB of RAM, and 410 GB of local storage, offering balanced multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D13 features 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 56 GB RAM, and 410 GB local storage, delivering average multi-core and database performance.
tagline: A balanced virtual machine combining dedicated Intel Xeon processing, generous memory capacity, and local storage for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz
56.0 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB per core
410 GB of local storage for temporary data and caching
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth in benchmarks
Suited for general-purpose, web serving, and database workloads
